title: Resting EEG

funding_references.funder_name: OPTIONAL. List of sources of funding (grant numbers)

description: A resting-state EEG dataset acquired from 128 channels at 1000 Hz sampling rate using an Electrical Geodesics amplifier system, complemented by structural MRI data from a GE MR750 scanner. The dataset includes preprocessed EEG signals with artifact removal and channel interpolation, as well as high-resolution anatomical imaging. This multimodal neuroimaging resource enables investigation of whole-brain electrophysiological functional connectivity dynamics during rest.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded using a 128-channel Amps 300 amplifier (Electrical Geodesics Inc.) at 1000 Hz sampling frequency during resting state. Preprocessing included band-pass filtering (0.5–100 Hz), notch filtering (58–62 Hz), artifact removal, bad channel interpolation, and re-referencing to average. Data were downsampled to 250 Hz. Structural MRI was acquired using a GE MR750 scanner with BRAVO sequence (FOV 240 mm, 180 axial slices).
